title: platyspermum littorale
intro: Platyspermum littorale, a plant species within the family Apiaceae, is commonly known as coastal hemlock or sea hemlock. It is native to the coastal regions of Australia and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in essential oils and have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Platyspermum littorale is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory and skin problems.
